Experienced practitioners therefore schedule the first annual sampling event as soon as frost leaves

as the analytical method itself. They routinely use low flow or passive sampling techniques to
avoid artificial turbidity spikes that can skew dissolved phase results for metals and PFAS,
and they deploy dedicated pumps or passive diffusion bags at least two weeks prior,
so that equilibration occurs under true ambient conditions. The most common mistake is treating

include a water level versus concentration plot in every annual performance report,
